session.tool_result:
18→When a plan becomes a PR, erk establishes a linkage that allows: 19→ 20→- `erk pr list` to show which plans have associated PRs 21→- PRs to automatically close their source plan when merged 22→- The 🔗 indicator to appear for auto-closing PRs 23→ 24→## How Linkages Are Created 25→ 26→### Via `erk pr submit` 27→ 28→The primary path for creating issue-PR linkages: 29→ 30→1. Creates branch from plan issue (e.g., `P123-feature-12-11-0948`) 31→2. Creates `.erk/impl-context/` folder with `ref.json` containing plan reference 32→3. Creates draft PR with `Closes #N` in **initial** body 33→4. GitHub registers the `CrossReferencedEvent` with `willCloseTarget: true` 34→ 35→**Key implementation**: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` 36→ 37→### Via `/erk:pr-submit` or `/erk:git-pr-push`
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

tool_use.param:
The primary path for creating issue-PR linkages:

The primary path for creating plan-PR linkages:
1. Creates branch from plan (e.g., `P123-feature-12-11-0948`)

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: **Important behavior**: GitHub's `willCloseTarget` field is set at PR creation time only. ...[truncated 711 chars]...t` passes the "Closes" text to `create_pr()` rather than adding it via `update_pr_body()`.

**Important behavior**: GitHub's `willCloseTarget` field is set at PR creation time only. ...[truncated 814 chars]... GitHub uses "issue" terminology internally, erk refers to these as "plans" or "plan PRs".

See erk-exec-commands.md Phase 5 Terminology Standardization." 130:erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521 # From plan number 144:- `--issue` - Plan number to fetch plan from 178:erk exec setup-impl --issue 2521 # From plan number 211:These commands were renamed from issue-oriented to PR-oriented terminology: 215:| `close-issue-with-comment` | `close-pr` | 216:| `plan-update-issue` | `plan-update` | 217:| `setup-impl-from-issue` | `setup-impl-from-pr` | 218:| `issue-title-to-filename` | `plan-title-to-filename` | 219:| `create-issue-from-session` | `create-pr-from-session` | 223:CLI flags and error codes were standardized from "issue" to "plan" terminology: 227:| `--plan-issue` (flag) | `--learn-plan` | 228:| `missing-plan-issue` (error code) | `missing-learn-plan` | 229:| `unexpected-plan-issue` (error code) | `unexpected-learn-plan` | 230:| `no-issue-reference` (error code) | `no-plan-reference` | 231:| `issue-not-found` (error code) | `plan-not-found` |

session.assistant:
Plan execution complete. Here's the summary:
**PR #8944**: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8944
**Changes made across 7 docs files:**
1. **`docs/learned/cli/plan-implement.md`** — Replaced "GitHub issues" → "GitHub", "Issue number" → "Plan number", "issue tracking" → "plan tracking", `has_issue_tracking` → `has_plan_tracking`, "plan issue" → "plan", and similar 2. **`docs/learned/cli/command-organization.md`** — Replaced all "plan issue" → "plan" in command descriptions and tables 3. **`docs/learned/cli/erk-exec-commands.md`** — Updated "plan issue metadata" → "plan metadata", "issue-based plans" → "plan-based implementations", and description text around `--issue` flag 4. **`docs/learned/cli/pr-submit-pipeline.md`** — Replaced "issue number" → "plan number", "issue linkage" → "plan linkage" 5. **`docs/learned/erk/issue-pr-linkage-storage.md`** — Title "Issue-PR Linkage" → "Plan-PR Linkage", replaced "issue" → "plan" throughout when referring to erk plans (kept GitHub API terminology where it refers to GitHub's native "issue" concepts) 6. **`docs/learned/erk/pr-address-workflows.md`** — Replaced `{issue}` placeholder → `{plan_number}`, "plan issue" → "plan", "GitHub issue" → "Plan" 7. **`docs/learned/erk/remote-workflow-template.md`** — Replaced "plan issue number" → "plan number", "on the issue" → "on the plan"
**Intentionally preserved "issue" references:** - Historical renaming tables in erk-exec-commands.md - `--issue` CLI flag (still the actual flag name in code) - `issue.json` legacy file name references - GitHub API terminology (auto-close behavior, timeline events) - `get_prs_linked_to_issues()` function name (not yet renamed in code)
